[h2]Summary;[/h2]  Do NOT purchase in it's current state at full price, or ever for that matter, get PD2 instead, and evaluate from there based on how much you like it's prequel.  If you insist on purchasing it, do it at 70% sale or more, ever since 2026 begun the playerbase has not broken through the 1000 player barrier on Steam, and at the time of writing this review, it's unlikely this will change since there's no significant changes planned in the roadmap.  For the ones who want a more fleshed out description of why i can't in good faith recommend this game in it's current state;     [h3]The Good;[/h3]  -Fresher take on Stealth as civilian mode allows for a more flexible playstyle and less black/white builds, doable even with loud-focused setups and flows nicely into the alarms going off.   -Generally satisfying weapon handling and visual customization, although their mechanical modification still needs a rework to be less abstract, for which small steps have been recently taken, such as the introduction of damage numbers in Options.  -Fleshed out mask customization by parts instead of as a whole, to the point of being reverse ported to PD2, however comes with the caveat of having to randomly drops the best looking paints on level up, which would be fixed by allowing their purchase with C-Stacks.     [h3]The Bad;[/h3]  -Lack of inventory management, one would think that dragging and dropping and reordering items/clothes/masks/weapons would be common sense in 2026, but it's not at the time of writing this.  -Little randomization from one instance of a heist to another, making for much less potential in replayability, if you came from PD2 you will feel your brain slowly atrophy as your need to adapt on the run to the unexpected gets cut in half.  -Overabundance of abstract objectives not generally signaled with a visual queue, which makes for a very confusing experience to new players, and hinders visibility of important mission equipment, bagged loot is barely identifiable through walls once you put 2+ floors between you and it, and specially so in the middle of combat, would greatly benefit from an outline update for objects, and automatic marking of objectives after a set amount of time, this is not new to PD2 and shouldn't be absent in PD3.   -There is arguably very little point in the grind, weapons need to be leveled up through use, but the reward for doing it is unrewarding as Weapon Modifications go from mildly neat to trivial. C-Stacks are likewise trivialized to get with the introduction of Daily Challenges and there's very little impact in what you can purchase with them after you get the cosmetics/masks/mods that you want, and Renown which is the equivalent to PD2's Infamy is completely unrewarding, lacking unique rewards whatsoever which makes the grind up to 1600 feel absolutely pointless unless you just want to see a number go up. PD2 allowed up to 500 Infamy levels, but at least the first 100 would give you unique rewards for your work, in PD3 all you get is randomized paint drops for your masks which expire on use.  -Tonally all over the place, originally went for a serious tone similar to PD: The Heist, which could have worked in it's own right, but since there was no significant resurgence in interest after so called "Operation: Medic Bag", SBZ had to press the emergency moneyprinter button AKA Jacket, so we have chicken-man back with his robot voice which is even more hit-or-miss than it used to be (some lines are raw and satisfying, but some are R*ddit tier), weapon stickers that look ported from the year 2006, and a growing collection of meme masks.     [h3]The Ugly;[/h3]  -Your savefile is Online only, you don't own your savefile like in PD2, it's all handled Online by the Nebula servers, which means that if the Nebula servers ever get put down, there goes all your progress, and this leads directly into the next point.  -No Offline mode, and SBZ still stringing the community along with false promises for it 2+ years later, it's development is officially shelved, likely as a last resort for the theoretical moment when Online servers/Starbreeze Nebula gets shutdown due to surmounting maintenance costs and a dwindling playerbase, add to this a dwindling number of developers after their recent January-March 2026 layoffs, and we don't even have Community Managers, since the role was tagged for redundancy, so communication is limited to "Social" managers like "PAYDAYgame" which are basically sanitized-corpo speak full of promises, and little substance.  -Last update was AI portraits, fat cops, and a lockpick mechanic that should have been in the original base game.  -Next update at the time of originally writing this review is going to be 4-5 meme masks; A paperbag, a bucket, a safe, a Dallas meme, and an arguably iconic mask from PD2 but that is only getting added because it got pushed on Twitter by a fan, so they want to latch on this small/nonexistant "hype", oh and allegedly P2P beta, but still with the Nebula servers in place. That's it.  -The main contractor, Shade, is an obnoxious quirkchungus r*dditor woman with anger issues that will make your eyes roll back inside your head faster than she can say "Yahtzee" or remind you that someone wants to "Piss in your Cheerios." We knew Bain/Simon Viklund was not coming back, but they arguably went for the worst possible alternative they found, she's not charming, she's not funny, she's not even positively memorable, and she will make your Final Charge an annoying nagging session with constant F-bombs drilling your cerebellum instead of the adrenaline pumping moment it should be.  -Tangential point, but if you're a collector/completionist, you'll never own all masks unless you happened to purchase the completely overpriced Collector's Edition at launch, for which there was almost no physical stock for PC, or unless you want to go through the trouble of purchasing a PS5 copy so SBZ support can feel useful for once in porting your console DLC to PC. There's also a good amount of preorder exclusive masks and a couple outfits no longer available for purchase, and several sets of Twitch rewards. (These at the very least will likely come back.)     [h3]TLDR;[/h3]  Truly, one of the most Starbreeze games of all time and the distillation of Bo Andersson's decade long power trip, don't waste your money until (if at all) there's an Offline mode, the few developers that remain are trying their best, but they are generally too scared to make significant changes due to previous backlash so new changes come across as too little-too late, and all the heavy hitters that made PD2 into a staple, like Ulf Andersson, Simon Viklund, and the community managers like Almir and Elisabeth among many others have either left, been pushed out, or rearranged to other less impactful positions.  Source: 4K Hours on PD2, and very well acquainted since 2015 with the SBZ seal of "quality", you can expect another OVK's TWD here in due time if they keep this unhealthy romance with their Online-only aspirations. My patience is at an end. The game developers are deliberately trying to ruin Payday 3. It has so much potential to be better than Payday 2. It got off to a bad start, but the developers have made it even worse.   It was fun at times, with the hope that the game would get better. But there are still many problems that haven't been solved, and more problems were added with each patch.   Especially the payday3.exe crash, which mainly occurs when the graphics card driver is not updated. But that's normal for UE4.   In Cook Off, I still have the problem that Shade skips her text. The ‘hold up’ could be swallowed and I would add the wrong ingredient because there is too long a pause before the next text.  Payday 3 has always had its ups and downs. The biggest highlights were the new heists. However, patch 2.5 was a big mistake, as you could only survive with a handful of builds. And with 3.2, more builds were nerfed, further limiting the selection (such as the sniper with Head Games Aces).  The game was fun at certain times. But now I don't enjoy it anymore.  Currently, the strongest build is the Sentry Gun build, which tempts players to bash the Sentry Gun like Neanderthals (ability: Repair Job) to repair it (sink heat). The kills come automatically and all you have to do is regulate the heat by hitting it!  During a 40-minute game in Cook Off with three other players in sentry build, I was hardly able to kill any opponents. The game was so buggy that every now and then, only 10 opponents spawned over a period of 2 minutes. What do you want to do with the game if you can only kill the snipers? In the end, you see that the best player has 1,500 more kills than you and feel useless.  There were so many requests to set up a feature that only allowed players above a certain level to participate in the heist. It's not right that low-level players are allowed to participate in every robbery. That ruined a few cook-off-sessions for me because they thought they could cook too.     I'm taking a break from this game. Let's see if it improves, but I don't think so.

As a long-time Payday 2 fan, I was eager to dive into Payday 3, but what I encountered was a frustrating experience marked by several missteps.    Always-Online Requirement: The mandatory online connection, even for solo play, felt like a betrayal. Many players, including myself, felt disempowered and disconnected, especially when server issues hit right at launch.    Challenge-Driven Progression: The new system ties character progression to specific challenges, and changes the joy of heisting into a tedious checklist. Completing a thrilling heist but gaining no progress because you didn’t tick off a challenge felt like turning gameplay into WORK - very boring WORK. This shift has led to my burnout very quickly and generally a loss of excitement :/    Clunky Movement: While the graphics are upgraded, the movement feels off. As a veteran of Payday 2, the rhythmic flow I’ve internalised feels disrupted, creating cognitive friction that detracts from the experience. It’s not broken, just frustratingly different.    Loss of Content: The game launched with significantly less content, only 8 heists instead of 12, along with fewer weapons. Losing features like Crime.net and the social aspects of crew planning has stripped away the franchise's core community spirit.    In conclusion, Payday 3 feels like an over-correction driven by corporate fears rather than a player-focused experience. The initial joy of heisting is overshadowed by feelings of loss and disconnection, it generally feels like a cash grab from the studio that they abandoned and went back to supporting Payday 2.

I understand why so many players are disappointed with Payday 3. It’s not a bad game, and I actually appreciate how easy it is to get into. You level up quickly, the systems are simple, and it’s clearly beginner-friendly. However, that accessibility mostly exists because Payday 3 offers far less depth than Payday 2. There are significantly fewer weapons, skill trees, and overall customization options. In fact, it sometimes feels even more barebones than Payday: The Heist, and that’s a serious problem.  The gameplay itself also feels outdated, like playing a game from around 2015 rather than a modern sequel. Melee attacks feel useless, and the skill trees lack impact and power, making progression feel underwhelming instead of rewarding.  Content is another major issue. There simply isn’t much of it, and two of the maps are essentially copy-pasted from Payday 2. Selling additional maps as DLC on top of this already thin package feels insulting - especially when the base game feels like an unfinished shell of a Payday title.  On top of all that, the online experience is almost nonexistent. The lobbies are dead and finding active players is difficult.  Overall, Payday 3 isn’t an awful game on its own, but compared to its predecessors, it’s a clear disappointment. It feels more like a cash grab than a worthy evolution of the series.

Game is ruined by microtransactions, lack of content, and stupid game mechanics.  PayDay 2 is one of my favorite games of all time, for some time I tried to beat every mission on every difficulty, one of the only games where I actually enjoy the difficult missions. But PayDay 3 is just an insult to the PayDay series. Barely any guns, barely any heists. ZERO melee weapons in the game, and using melee on cops with your weapon *literally* does nothing at all, like..... huh? Although there are barely any guns, even when you do unlock a new gun, you have to restart your game just to use it. This game is PURELY online, no mods allowed, no solo play/coop play allowed, they just started an open beta for solo play, but it's still riddled with online only features. When you go to buy a new weapon or armor, you get paid DLC items at the top of the list, then scrolling down far you finally see weapons you can buy. Weapon customization is laughably bad, there's barely any options to choose from, and the only options you do have, you have to use the gun for around 30 heists just to unlock them. Something PayDay 2 did kind of bad, is enemy spawning, where enemies can spawn 10 feet away from you, but this game....... does it 10x worse. Enemies can spawn under cars near you, and they come from vents above you. My ONLY deaths in this game, are from cloakers spawning 3 feet away from me, and playing their attack sound as they are already pinning me. Cops spawning from under cars makes me feel like I am playing a zombie game, was there a manhole under the car, how did they get there? I can go on and on about how the guns in the game are horrible, but I'll just use one example: The first DMR you get, one of the only DMRs, is not a one shot headshot and only has a 12 round mag, so even if you're a great shot, and get NOTHING but headshots, you still have to reload constantly after getting *maybe* 5-6 kills, and that's not taking into account that the cops dodge and crouch constantly. I probably see cops crouching in place with no cover, more then I see them using cover or moving up. The best weapon in the game is the starter pistol :) Just because its a two shot headshot, and reloads fast.     TLDR;  Don't buy this mess of a broken game, built on nostalgia and hype from the previous game. That has no content what so ever, yet already has several DLC.

This game used to be one of the most fun in my library.  I can not recommend this game because the publisher and studio have changed it so much from the original proposition that it has become a different product entirely. Since these changes were implemented many hours after my purchase, I have no recourse (very tricky "bait and switch," Starbreeze Entertainment; you got us good).  Having played since the original Payday, one of the main attractions for many of us has been well-planned, suave, and low-key heists. No alarms, no killing. If we do our job right, nobody even knows we were there.  The choice to "go loud" or assault the heist has always been available and exhilarating. Perhaps a heist goes wrong, and we have to bargain or shoot our way out. This represents everything the game is supposed to and has always offered until now: a choice.  With the introduction of the Skills 2.0 update, intellect, sophistication, and patience have been replaced with mindless, repetitive levels of pointing and clicking at "spongy" puppets.  Ocean's Eleven has become Triple 9.

I bought this with a 50% discount for 11.50 and I can still confidently say it was not worth it. Firstly, there's about 10-12 heists without DLC, and one, ''First World Bank'', has already existed in previous titles. Too little content for the price tag. There's also way less weapon choice compared to Payday 2, which costs 10-15 dollars at most. Secondly, I could only bear playing two heists, and I averaged a single-digit 9 FPS. I even turned on Frame Gen, which did absolutely nothing. Thirdly, my PC doesn't even stutter when I hit a wall going 200 mph in BeamNG.drive 0.38, and I get 6 times less FPS when I'm just sitting in front of a bank doing absolutely nothing. It is not worth paying the full price of 23 USD or even a discount for 9 FPS. Low end PC owners, buy it on console instead. Also, I can't even run this same game on my Xbox Series S in Quality mode without frame drops and Performance mode makes it look terrible.

PAYDAY 3 is, in a sentence, a hollow, soulless shell of what made PAYDAY great. Obviously, it's all subjective, but the paltry player count seems to indicate some truth to these assertations: PAYDAY 3 is much worse than PAYDAY 2 in almost every way. The shooting feels better, movement feels a bit less floaty and less cartoonish, and the graphics look better. Outside of those things? There's really nothing that I can recommend a PAYDAY 2 player or even an PAYDAY: The Heist player about in regards to PAYDAY 3.  PAYDAY 2 has: - More content (it's been out longer, so that makes sense) - Better gameplay loops - Better heists (in most cases, at least) - More weapon variety - More characters - Better fleshed out characters (even the returning characters in 3 have less character than their PD2 selves) - Better build variety (a thing that is now woefully apparent with the recent patch to the relatively new Skills 2.0 update in PD3) - Offline capabilities and no always-online requirement - A feeling of heart and soul that is almost entirely absent from the 3rd game  If you want a PAYDAY game, play 2 for wacky heisting fun and 1 for a more grounded experience.

i really really wanted to have hope that this game would be good. and after its launch i still wanted to have hope that it could be good someday but my optimism is just long gone atp.   i think the game does make improvements over 2 in some ways. the general flow of combat feels a bit better. the gunplay in particular is a decent upgrade. the enemies feel a bit better to fight too (the bar is in the deep pits of hell in that regard though and they're still quite stupid and not really what they should be). it also looks nicer though i wish it had a bit more flavor to it. the animations are a huge improvement and you can tell the animator has fun working on them. [spoiler] SUPER TRUTH NUKE: theres no h3h3 or bomb forest or goat sim so its the better game. refute me on this one. you cant :) [/spoiler]  the issue is that everything else just kinda sucks honestly.  progression sucks and theres no endgame, no prestige system like infamy in the second game. the ui is better than it was at launch but still sort of a mess. the gun customization is cool visually but its practically just cosmetic as the stats dont matter on guns. you also have to level them up for some reason which ive never really understood. the arsenal as a whole is a giant downgrade, lacking the weird and wacky weapons the 2nd game had (you're telling me we didn't get THE JUDGE until over a year and a half in?) the game being always online means that stuff that just worked without a problem before like creating lobbies or buying attachments now takes quite a bit of time (its insane that it still takes over a minute to get into a lobby for a heist that YOU created).  the heists are just okayyyyy at best. the more recent ones have been a huge regression back to launch payday 2 era heists where you just bag a bunch of stuff up and leave with little depth outside of it. theres just not much spectacle anymore outside of a rare moment where you do something cool (like taking out an ai or crashing the stock market). only recently have we begun getting rng in heists which was a fundamental part of the 2nd game and again its just in heists where you bag ♥♥♥♥ and leave.   buildcrafting is pretty unintuitive and a giant step down from 2. the first iteration of skills in this game were awful and had no standouts and nothing interesting. the second iteration is more interesting but still lacks the most fun stuff from 2 like jokers and crits. also the aforementioned cosmetic attachments on guns. admittedly i havent played much with skills 2.0 but thats because theres nothing to use those builds in.  which brings me to my last point which is that theres nothing to do. once again theres no endgame and little variety in the heists themselves. the cadence of content in the game is pretty bad too. post wh 2 worked on a 3-4 month schedule where one month a cosmetic pack would come out, the next month a weapon pack, and then finally ended off with a heist. this was a good release cadence and it has been completely messed up here. we get a new bag moving simulator heist every 4-6 months now and maybe the occasional new weapon but thats it. the most recent heist at the time of writing (shopping spree) came out about 4 months after the last one and contains nothing but the heist itself. theres a bunch of new weapons they modeled but they arent usable and are instead just for show on a wall in a gunshop. this is not necessarily the actual devs fault and is rather on management for giving them a budget of $10 a year because year 1 had actual content updates drop at an okay pace (though it was pretty rocky the first few months iirc)  theres just so much wasted potential here. if starbreeze got their heads out of there asses and locked in and gave the devs a budget above $10 past year 1 maybe things would be different. never say never i suppose but i really think the games just cooked now and it probably wont make it past this year.  at least it doesn't crash every 10 seconds i suppose?

This is just no longer the game I even purchased and is riddled with cripplingly bad balancing decisions that are fueled entirely by Reddit being terrible at video games.  Every single update just reduces any difficulty the game has further and further, to the point it just does not exist. Mechanics are inserted haphazardly without any consideration for the game design. Over half the paid DLC Heists are terrible and not worth any money. The core progression is terrible and just lacks a metaphorical carrot on a stick to keep you playing. Skills 2.0 unironically ruined the game, it removed multiple playstyles and one I liked immensely. STOP LISTENING TO KKNOWLEY STARBREEZE There is so much sustain that failing heists requires effort to even do. Armor 2.0 was terrible and I'm not gonna be gaslighted into thinking otherwise. Resources legit don't matter in this game, thanks Starbreeze. Literal 2 Second God Mode was left in the game on demand for over a month, thanks Starbreeze. Bulldozers are more of a joke than they are in PayDay 2 (how do you manage to accomplish this, Starbreeze). Heists have 0 replayability or replay value, half of them are also terrible in terms of objective design. Guns still take forever to unlock attachments on, because you have to individually level up your guns by spamming out heists. PDTH you leveled guns by doing challenges & stuff, not mindlessly spamming out heists. The player base doesn't like Shade and would just rather have Locke as our handler. Random characters we've never met before are now our contractors with 0 explanation as to how they know Shade. Offline Mode is never gonna happen, Starbreeze doesn't have the money for it. Overkill Weapons are boring, they don't actually create variety and you just pick them based on preference.  TL;DR They literally have been through 4 separate game directors, have terrible balancing decisions, take Reddit's feedback to heart (Reddit doesn't know how to even play the game) and leave disgustingly broken things in the game for upwards of months that would get patched out in a week in any other game. Its just not worth your money, most of the DLC content is bad and they're planning on putting this game onto Unreal Engine 5 (it is gonna run worse when that happens).

It's a decent game but a dissapointing sequel unfortunately even after all these major updates. The game has a long way to go till it reaches the status of Payday 2 and it feels like it was rushed the same way as CP2077.  - Armor QoL is there and works out okay compared to the old system. - Skills 2.0 could use another revamp. With the old skill system gone this needs to be improved even further as lot of skills still feel barebones or irrelevant. - Market is literally a mess when it comes to finding primary/secondary weapons and its type while in PD2 it had weapons categorized properly on whether it's an Assault Rifle or a Marksman, not to mention that Primary/Secondary had their own unique weapons while here you can go to your Secondary slot and it allows you to buy a Primary weapon but it won't appear as Secondary weapon which can be confusing if you missed the highlighted text above the weapon. The same applies for when it comes to Masks and Outfits as well. Hoping they bring back the old system of PD2. - Weapon stats is something that's being mentioned since a long time and we want the numbers to be back instead of sliders.  - Weapon upgrades to unlock mods is super boring as well for people who prefer to try out different weapons/builds. - Challenge tokens should be used the same way as Continental tokens to unlock weapon mods or masks like in PD2. As of now it's limited on what you can buy with them from weapons to masks. - Favors needs to be changed and bring Preplanning back. Having to buy favors from the shop and only be allowed to place 1 per person makes no sense, even more if you forget to buy favors beforehand from the market. - DLCs are okay but the objectives don't capture the same feeling of PD2 in terms of satisfaction. - I wish the game remained P2P because even as a host you can have desync with the game having official servers now that's Online Only, plus the fact that it takes a while to match you into a server whether you play solo or duo is annoying.  Overall the game is improving but it's in dire need of the same QoL updates as of PD2, literally needs to become as close to PD2 as possible and people will be back playing since both the gameplay and animations are very improved and it can be fun with the new engine. However for now I will only recommend to grab it on sale for 15$ since unfortunately it feels very empty as of now, both in maps and weapon/customization choices let alone the bugs and some annoying features it has.

After 2 years I am still a bit dissapointed. The core mechanics are there okay, some new textures, weapons and overall improvement to the game but it still does not feel like "Payday 3" as a good newcomer for the good "Payday 2" game. During holidays I finally wanted to enjoy some other games and after the bad start 2 years ago I tried to give it a chance at all. Well, after playing some missions and spend some hours to the game I can say that they improved things (that were messed up at the beginning) but there are still some lacks: - bot commands/bot AI is still so annoying.. if you play solo they still run towards you and idk why you still have no opportunity to hold bots at a certain spot (e.g. to wait on a specific spot) - the overhauled skill system is new and sure its better than the beginning ones, but there is still a lack on content to personalize it more - huge lack of content: after 2 years there are still a few maps and just couple new ones (DLCs) for which you have to pay for. For me it feels like just a money grabbing, greeding move and not the expected "Payday 3" content everyone wished - there are way to less weapons for beginners.. after 2 years playing Payday 3 there is literally one weapon to choose and to play for (idk) the first 15-20 levels? give beginners the opportunity to move forward, unlock more weapons and to "buy" extensions e.g. silencers at the beginning. It is quite impossible to play stealth for beginners which is quite annoying - performance is improved but I got some hitches even with a 9800x3d +rtx 5080 but its way better compared to the messy beginning xD  After all: In the current state I would not recommend this game. Maybe wait some months, maybe a year after there is more content available, hopefully "free content like masks, weapons and maps".

Payday 3 is a good game, but it removed too much.    They did a lot correct with its stealth system; they majorly overhauled it and made it much more fun to do a mission in stealth, and the Payday team knows this because they request content creators to showcase this when sponsored to play the game.    A major mistake The Payday team made was that they removed too much, especially from the loadout menu. Payday 2 had a much larger variety of weapons, including akimbo (duel-wielded) weapons than Payday three, and the system of buying weapons is through vendors, but the way they organize the weapons is messy and confusing. They also removed a major mechanic from the game, perk decks, which  the lack thereof ruined my enjoyment of the game. Additionally, the Payday team decided to completely remove all melee customization and effectively made melee useless, which I was very disappointed to learn.    Overall, the game is very fun if you want to play a stealth/puzzle game, but as a horde shooter, it massively lets down the player due to the lack of choice in weapons and skills.

This was the only game I ever preordered because of how much I loved Payday 2. I figured "Hey, they had over a decade of mistakes, lessons, and failed projects to build off of, surely they know what they're doing by now." Yeah, that sentiment aged well.  The game we ended up with at launch was a horrible, quarter baked, miserable excuse for a sequel. At this point in the game's life, it has only just gotten around to fixing up the horrendous launch state of the skill system. The weapons themselves are still underwhelming and fail to show useful stats, not to mention the mediocre feel of their attachments. Enemy AI is still braindead and somehow feel worse than they used to at launch. The technical side of the game is an absolute mess, with some day 1 issues such as civilians clipping through floors and the unreasonably unstable servers still being just as bad as they were 2 years ago. DLCs are still costly and there's little content aside from quarterly releases, leaving the game in extended droughts. Not to mention the AI slop they've dropped on social media, they seem to be doing the best they can to ruin the game as much as possible. I think Payday 3 is fun. I think it has improved quite a lot since launch. However, I cannot, in good conscience, give this game a recommendation.  I have been patient with this game. I've played it despite its flaws, despite being a clearly inferior game to both its predecessors. I've waited through all the controversies for the game to get better. And it has gotten better: we've had Armor 2.0 which actually made armor interesting to use, and we've also had Skills 2.0, which removed the asinine Edge/Grit/Rush system and completely revamped a lot of the skills in a way that made the game much more fun to play... in loud in particular. Yeah, sorry stealth players...  But every time Payday 3 updates, I feel like the monkey paw curls one of its fingers. A big update happened? We're also cancelling offline mode! New heist? By the way, Almir Listo, the most recognizable face of Payday himself, leaves the company! Skills 2.0 update came out? Here, buy our plushie and look at our AI slop! Also, that's the only content you're getting for the rest of the year. Merry flocking Christmas heisters!  And those are just a few of the ones that I remembered off the top of my head. It just doesn't stop. This game is cursed to take a step in the right direction, only to jump right into traffic and get hit by a truck. The Advent Calendar event is the straw that broke the camels back for me that made me write this review. How do you make a 25-day event with content for every day, and deliver just one update of in-game content? The amount of teasers we were getting kind of led me to think we might actually get a heist. And my expectations were as low as low can be by this point. When the event began, I thought we'll get the Skills 2.0 update and a gun; not even a heist. [i]We didn't even get that.[/i]  And this is a relatively small gripe, but why is Teddy Moo so damn forced all of a sudden? In the almost 15 year history of this franchise, Teddy Moo made his debut in No Mercy in PD:TH, he re-emerged in the remake of No Mercy for PD2, then he got a mask in PD2 and an outfit in PD3. That's four times he's even mentioned in the franchise, and only two of which where he's actually played a role in a heist (both of which are the same heist in different games, mind you), in almost 15 years! And now you're expecting everyone to just accept him as the new mascot of the Payday franchise? Get out of here with that contrived nonsense! Let me give you four words that you could work with to use as mascots: "Dallas," "Hoxton," "Wolf," and "Chains." The original four heisters, the ones who's faces are all over this entire franchise, aren't good enough anymore?  So, no. I cannot recommend this game. Not until Starbreeze and OVK get it together and actually focus on making the game better and not showing half-hearted efforts to appeal to your fans in trying to keep the franchise alive by any means necessary. The 180 they have to pull on this game has to be on the level of Cyberpunk 2077 or No Man's Sky, but I really don't see that happening.  I'm otherwise a relatively patient person. I've been waiting for a sequel to something I love for well over a decade now, and I thought I could wait until Payday 3 could get to the point where I can happily recommend it to my friends. Unfortunately, this game has stretched my patience so thin that I don't think that day will ever come. So all I can do, at this point, is to keep following this game until the inevitable death knell rings.  ... At least we still have Payday 2.

As a massively obsessed fan of PD:TH and PD2, PD3 is an absolute disappointment compared to its predecessors even after 2 years of its release. Game lacks so much content in every department, many features that made PD2 as fun as it is replayable are missing such as perk decks, and the overall experience was just a massive "Meh".   Here were the things that PD3 improved from PD2:  -Visuals are obviously a massive improvement. Texture details are actually detailed. Environments feel more vibrant and life-like, and textures don't look like MS Paint generated geometrics anymore.   -Gunplay feels so nice and punchy. I feel the weight and "oomph" of shooting, and the sound design and animations of the gunplay are definitely satisfactory. The way guns feel here actually feels similar to Battlefield 6 for me, for some reason.  -Movement is a lot more fluid and responsive. I feel fast when I sprint, and I go wherever I input myself to go. No issues on movement. Also, instead of spamming sprint+jump+crouch in PD2, they added sliding, which is neat.  -Interaction animations are cool. Every specific interaction depending on the object or task has their own specific animation, which I thoroughly enjoyed. It isn't that static no-hands display on every interaction like PD2 anymore.  -Overkill weapons are a nice touch.  -Stealth is SO MUCH BETTER. You can finally interact and do what you need to do without the need to mask in. Guards feel more natural, and they don't phase through solid walls anymore or see you through 2 layers of concrete to detect you. Also, being able to answer the pager while carrying the body? I love it.  -The game does feel more "high-octane" than PD2, which I do enjoy and expected for PD3. It feels more adrenaline-inducing, and I do still get that iconic rush of a Payday game.  -Haven't encountered any bugs so far, so I assume most the bugs on release were already addressed by now.  Here are the things PD3 has downgraded from PD2:  -Obviously, content. Ever 2 years later from release, there is still an absolute content drought. Fewer heists than PD2 even on that game's release. There are way too few guns to play with, and even with the new Skill Tree 2.0 update, the skill trees still feel bare and minimum to me. The sandbox mayhem you could enjoy from PD2 is missing in PD3. Also, no perk decks. What the ♥♥♥♥?  -Heists feel way too short. I noticed that in a span of one hour, I was able to finish 5 heists. That's roughly 12 minutes only per heist. Compared to PD2, where a heist can take as long as 45 minutes, even one day heists. Speaking of, please bring back multi-day heists.  -Heists weren't memorable at all. A number of PD2 heists actually had weight to them, and some of them stuck to me to this day, such as Hoxton breakout, Hotline Miami, Birth of Sky, Shadow Raid, and many more. In PD3, I play through a heist, and I don't feel much anything afterwards. I just felt the heists were mostly just a get-in, smash some things and take loot, and escape. Them being short as well added to their blandness. They just didn't feel good.  -Heist mechanics are okay. Still has the usual do this and do that to open this and access that type of flow. I say downgrade in the sense that there isn't any improvement from PD2. However, the heists mostly lack on the random nature that PD2 heists have especially on things like objective item spawns, making the replayability of each heist in PD3 just boring and static. I'm just doing and expecting the same thing over and over again.  -Difficulty? Where's that? I don't know, I only reached level 29 but I'm already able to breeze through overkill difficulty even without a completed skill tree. The game definitely feels WAY TOO EASIER. I noticed that, atleast for me, in increasing difficulty, the only thing that also increases is just the number of spawns. Damage felt more or less the same across all difficulties. The game didn't feel challenging enough for me to enjoy. You know the posture where you start leaning forward in your seat and focus your eyes to the monitor and lock in? I never experienced it in my 4 hours at the time of writing this review, even playing on Overkill difficulty.  -Police AI feels stale. I don't know how to describe it exactly, but they just feel more bland and unengaging. I don't get that same feeling of being overwhelmed like in PD2. It's like they only just spawn, and move a bit and shoot, as if they only exist just for something for you to left click at. Even special enemies, especially bulldozers, feel underwhelming.  -No infamy or prestige system, which adds to it's already poor replayability.  -PD3 is slowly developing a DLC infestation like PD2. The new content in this game aren't being released as free updates, but as all DLC. The recent DLC, for example, is just a heist. And it's cost at full price is not justified at all. I hope starbreeze makes future content mostly free updates, with a only a bare minimum of paid DLCs here and there if they need to.  -Due to the aforementioned downgrades I noticed, as well as many other factors, PD3 has an abysmal player count even with crossplay on. On the server browser, I usually find like only 8-ish heist lobbies every refresh. Sure that's good enough to find lobbies, and I do still get players joining my lobby when I host. However, in some heists I just end up playing with the same people.  There definitely are many other more issues with the game, but these were just the things that really made the game worse for me.  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------  I have played both PD:TH and PD2 for an unhealthy amount of hours, and they were the best CO-OP shooters I have ever played, with most of my hours sunk into PD2 despite it's poorly optimized, DLC infested, unbalanced content. Still, it was peak co-op heisting for me.  When the first trailer for Payday 3 was dropped, being hyped was an absolute understatement. I couldn't wait to get my hands on Payday 3 and see what Starbreeze has added to the heisting scene, seeing as how successful Payday 2 was. However, I have a personal rule that I usually wait 2 months after a game's release to see how well it performs, and usually 2 months is a good enough time to assess player retention after release. I was honestly fighting the biggest urge to purchase the game on release as I really just wanted to jump into the game immediately. Oh boy, was I right not to surrender myself to temptation.  In a nutshell, the game flopped HARD on release. The game got "Mostly Negative" reviews, and everyone, including my favorite PD content creators such as GeneralMcBadass, said the game was a buggy, content-deficient, lackluster experience. The first few months of the game was horrible, as what I've seen online.  I decided to let the game refine itself for a year before deciding to buy the game, as I had hoped by then that Starbreeze would have enough time to address all the issues. One year later, game still has ♥♥♥♥ reviews with mostly the same concerns such as poor content. So I gave it another year.  I mostly forgot about the game, and only remembered it this winter sale. Now the reviews improved, but it's only Mixed. From what I've heard, the recent update on skill trees was a step in the right direction, as well as people saying it's a lot better than release. So, seeing as it was 50% off anyways, I decided to grab it. Played 4 hours worth, and I did not thoroughly enjoy those 4 hours.  What happened, Starbreeze? PD3 had SO MUCH potential to be one of the greatest in the CO-OP genre. PD2's formula was a smash hit, so it came to no surprise that the expectations for PD3 were quite high. But by God, was this game an absolute underdelivery. I still have glimmers of hope that maybe you could turn this around, as I am among this franchise's most loyal and absolute fans. The games were my childhood, but even someone like me can't defend this slop.

The game itself is fine; I’d even say it is fun. It is definitely better than I expected, coming from Payday 2. After all, all you hear about this game is bad things. I would love to recommend this game, but there is one thing that absolutely destroys it.  The absolutely awful matchmaking/lobby system destroys the game. It is the worst I have ever seen in my entire life. Downright unusable! Every single developer who worked on this part of the game should simply be ashamed of themselves.  I’ll simply list a few bullet points that make this system unusable:  - Can’t chat in the lobby when in the main menu (this is comparable to an empty lobby in PD2).  - Can’t see what the host is doing when in the main menu (is he AFK, is he choosing a heist, etc.?).  - Can’t invite people to the lobby once a heist is selected but the heist hasn’t started yet.  - Can’t change lobby permissions (private, public, etc.) mid-game.  - Can’t set drop-in permissions, making stealth lobbies pointless.  - Can’t set level limits.  - The game uses two names in-game: the Starbreeze Nebula name and the Steam/Xbox/PSN name. It’s very confusing, as sometimes it shows the Starbreeze name and sometimes the Steam name.  - Can’t see what heist someone is on in Steam.  - Lobbies dissolve by default after a heist, making it difficult to stay with a competent team once you’ve found one.  - Can’t always see the username of the host in Crime.net.  I cannot for the life of me understand how a studio that made multiple top-tier co-op games completely forgot how to program proper online connectivity. If this game simply had the Payday 2 lobby system, I could recommend it.  Also, the community of this game is the awful. After owning the game for two weeks I play better than most people I encounter online, including high infamy players, at least on stealth. This would never be the case in Payday 2. In Payday 2 you would encounter lot's of good players.

So I don't usually write reviews to game unless they really impress me or they disappoint me completely, sadly, this is one of the later cases so let me begin with my issues towards the game:  - I wasn't really waiting for this game to be honest, I was thinking from the beginning it's a bad idea to push out another game when payday never needed a "new game", it just needed some update to it's second game to keep it up to date visually and mechanically.  - I've played payday 2 for a very long time and it's been my favourite game and first game on steam, I made a steam account just for payday and since then even if they had some attempts at greedyness, I loved every second of it, bought every dlc individually, was there for all the new update, everything new added to the game, hearing about payday 3 made me think they are giving up on payday 2 entirely which they didn't because they added a monthly subscription to give all the dlcs while having the monthly subscription, so I guess ♥♥♥♥ the hundreds of dollars I gave for dlcs cuz now everybody can have everything for just 9.99 euros a month... ofc after you buy the base game.  - I understand thet they wanted to make the game more "realistic" but they somehow managed to make it completely out of immersion, the arcadey payday 2 had me more immersed than this game ever had, I still come in every update thinking "even payday 2 had a bad start but they improved" but every update I'm trying to find reasons to not quit the game and sticking to payday 2 but I'm finding it almost impossible  - servers and network are a joke, why did they ever think even solo mode needs to find a server to host the game is beyond my understanding, feels like they got their server knowledge from ubisoft and they still somehow made it worse than them.  - I don't really have anything good to say about the game, it's mechanics are dreadful, it's gameplay is boring, the updates are exciting but then you get disappointed because the delivery is lackluster or doesn't feel like it improved on anything, I always liked starbreeze and tried finding a excuse for them but at this point, they had the whole community supporting them when they almost went bankrupt and then they still treat their costumers the way they do, I understand they need a source of income to pay people and resources, but at this point I'd rather have starbreeze focus on other projects and just admit payday franchise is just dead, they don't deserve your money or attention anymore, keep your money and spend it on something else.

"Hey Guys, wanna try that new payday game that came out?" > Den of Wolves isn't out yet, is it? "No I meant Payday 3! Silly." > You mean the "payday" game that I had to kill thousands to level a single weapon a bit, and had to do that on repeat just to have a skill tree? "Nooo, they fixed that! Now you level normally!" > Ohh, so just the game where armor is a finite and ♥♥♥♥ resource to manage then? "Nope, we fixed that too with Armor 2.0 (finally!)" > Really? Neat! So the guns don't feel so bad, the armor is decent, and this 'edge/grit/rush' system is...manageable, actually! Depending on how you approach it you can make a bunch of varied options and builds for specific situations...Maybe this isn't quite as bad as everyone said it was! and the unmasked/stealth/hostages aspect is a lot better than 2's!  [Fast Forward to December, 2025]  Hey, here's this new system that our survey says EVERYONE loves more than edge grit and rush! > looks inside > Every synergy or build concept (that Bias: I enjoyed) is now divided over 2-3 subsections, stuffed with a ton of filler combat perks for specific weapons/grenades, and that's it.  > You need to be level 150 for the *51* perk points, basically half of which you spend to acquire *2*, arguably 3 actual perks that benefit stealth (pre-mask/pre-cop) gameplay, and the other half being nerfed hacker/  severely nerfed grifter (getting to that). > Oh, did you enjoy having smoke grenades? That's the Ninja tree now. You'd think a Ninja would have throwing knives, but sorry, that's lumped in with Medic/Grifter. Want to pair engineering with a chunky rifle for killing Dozers? I hope you didn't want armor, or hacking with that, because you're already splitting between 2 if not 3 "classes". Oh, you *like* hostage taking? And the only grenade that helps with civy management is flashbangs? Well- do you like *ammo boxes?!* because that's the synergy with Hostage Taking!  > Transporter, Adrenaline, Grifter, Thief, Marks, Takedowns, Quickdraw/Desperado pistol setups- ENTIRE trees are smashed down into 1 or 2 perks, usually far weaker than whatever they offered before.   "But you have 51 skill points now, instead of 28 before!"  > I can, if I trim out every quality of life, recover 50-60% of the build(s) I enjoyed before, and almost none of what I can recover or keep pertains to pre-loud gameplay. And now the game expects me to let it control my loadout, because "Why would you ever want Shotgun perks as a hostage taker, or assault rifle perks with some medical fallback" > Did you want to be able to move loot good for your crew? *20+* of your 51 points are spent on acquiring *just* the ability to carry 2 bags without penalty, and quickly jimmy/punch a lock. Yeah, you get some benefits to armor bags and thicker armor! ...and also dodge!....which wants you.. to have...less armor...  It feels like the devs for Payday called up Cyberpunk and asked if they could copy the homework. The New Tree shows some promise, and maybe with enough care and effort you could build something fun or at least funny.  Hey, at least we'll actually get more deployable bags than just what we start with, that could at least be cool!  (Read the patch notes, they already have that skill on the chopping block for "2.1", along with some other nerfs pre-planned with "community approval/suggestion")  Yes, I saw you can throw a knife while masked off. Can't move the guard's body, though, unless they let you carry corpses unmasked, and losing a lot of camera control or stealth movement from *entire trees* that provided assets to stealth being squashed or removed...  I'd be more willing to consider this new system if they give us an extra 24 points, making it 75- that would let you get *most* of 3 separate trees of your choice [u] regardless [/u] of Starbreeze's idea of what makes a good synergy; and giving back that build freedom to choose your weapons based on what you enjoy, not what the game says you should. It would allow a smidge of wiggle-room for such overpowered perks as "You don't have to play our lockpicking minigame" without spending 1/5 of your character just to get that skill, or making those 7 point investments to "capstone" a tree, more valuable.   With how much the skills cost now though, and how utterly ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stealth feels/looks at current, I can't defend this game any more. Not when it feels like Payday is telling me how I'm allowed to heist. I'm tired of games telling me how they want me to play them. This ♥♥♥♥ sucks.

[h1] PAYDAY 3 takes a giant step away from its predecessor... and falls down the stairs, Peter Griffin style. [/h1]  Is the game fun to play when it works? Kinda. The game got better after the release with the introduction of reworked (PAYDAY 2-like) armor and skill systems. Stealth got more mechanically complicated with the introduction of different types of zones (Public, Private, and Secure), different types of civilians (normal Pedestrians and Employees), and the new Search phase that is now triggered first before the alarm (which throws out of the window all timings and paths of guards, so you have to be much more careful). And switching the difficulty feels more impactful than in the 2nd part, where playing on Mayhem is pretty much the same as on Death Sentence.   Loud also feels very different. Shooting got some weight added to it, SWAT units actually try to hide behind Shields, Cloakers are like toddlers with knives running around, Bulldozers can be a problem when there are a lot of enemies around, and Tasers... well, they are also here. On top of that there are 2 more new special units (Grenadier that throws toxic grenades that damage your health directly, and Drone Controller that hides somewhere in a corner and keeps launching multiple kinds of drones), which is nice, but in general the Loud part of the game is quite easy. Even on higher difficulties with randoms you can escape with a stealth build once one of them triggers the lasers in a vent where they had absolutely nothing to do.   But the rest of it, outside of the gameplay, is still broken and much worse than the second part. First of all, the game is online-only. Allegedly, to prevent players from cheating (you can check videos on YouTube to see how "well" that is working). Does it introduce problems? Of course it does! - Creating a contract takes 30+ seconds. - Restarting the contract doesn't change anything (same spawns of items, same codes, same everything), most likely because of the previous point.  - If the server crashes (and it will) - the host will also get disconnected. - If joining a lobby fails - sometimes it takes time to go back to the [noparse]Crime.net[/noparse], sometimes you have to "re-login".  And the UI isn't much better: - In-lobby chat is somehow worse than in the 2nd part. - There is no level filter for your lobbies. Low levels will frequently join Overkill Stealth just to start blasting guards (and playing solo is boring). - As far as I can see, you cannot join random lobbies with the DLC heists you don't own. You can still see them in the [noparse]Crime.net[/noparse], but instead of the "Join" button, there is a banner that tells you to buy the DLC, which is just scummy in my opinion. You can disable specific heists in the filters... but they reset each time you open the menu, so that's useless. - In the inventory, instead of pages of weapons or masks, we now have a scrollable list with 2 columns, which is incredibly annoying to use and shows only 8 items at once. - Weapon upgrading also uses a horizontal scrollable list, that is way too wide. - When buying a weapon - for some reason it shows you a list of all of them (both Primary and Secondary). In general some of the menus feel like a mobile game menus that weren't properly ported to PC.  So while I'm mostly having fun with the game, I wouldn't recommend it to my friends. It took them 2 years to fix only some of the launch problems, and with their new CEO being some EA executive who uses GenAI, I don't really want to support them anymore.

Payday 3 - is a perfect example of when developers take on too much and promise too much, but end up delivering something that's just “ well, whatever.”  To give you an overview of the scale of the game's failure, the second part continues to be played by an average of several tens of thousands of players, while this part does not even reach approximately 1,000 players.  Mods saved the game: people fixed everything that the developers had ruined and added everything that should have been in the game from the start. So what now? A huge number of mods are broken after almost every update, and on top of that, with the developers' arrogance and disregard for player feedback about what needs to be done with the game, modders are practically all giving up and abandoning their mods.  Optimization + UE 5 = no comment. While they started to fix everything with optimization, the network side is just terrible. Finding a lobby is impossible, connecting is usually impossible, and even if you do manage to connect, you'll most likely get kicked at the start or during the match. There are still a lot 8hit ton of bugs in the game.  A lot of features have been removed from the game, with only a few really good new ones added. At the start, there weren't even simple notifications about loot when it was handed over, no chases, no multi-part robberies, no safe houses, no lore, no restarts or pauses during robberies - although they did add some of this, it took them over a year just to add a couple of buttons and notifications.  The game was simply unplayable from the start, with no solo mode - now it's there, but it's still in beta. There is no content in the game, even though it has been in development since 2016. At the start, there were 8 heists, and in 2 years, there are now slightly more than 10. There are very few weapons in it. For some reason, they removed all melee weapons. There are no new enemies in the game, and there are fewer of them than in the previous part. In fact, the only truly new ones are the grenadier and the drone operator, and they removed all the variability depending on the difficulty level. So they also cut down on the number of difficulty levels.  Two years have passed, nothing has changed, the developers continue to make promises, but in the end they don't keep them or violate all acceptable deadlines. The person in charge has changed to Paul Keslin - it is highly unlikely that he will be able to fix or change anything. In fact, the developers need to completely redo the game from scratch and add about x10 times the original amount of content to make the game at least somewhat engaging.  To summarize, Payday 3 is a complete failure, with almost no chance of improvement, no matter how much you want it. Just please, don't buy this game, it's not worth it. You'll only be feeding these careless developers, who, instead of fixing the game, have ALREADY started adding DLC for characters and robberies. I have only one thing that I can say about the game: "An example of greedy corporations whose product has no right to exist due to such a disrespectful behavior towards the community."

Payday 3 is objectively worse than Payday 2. I will change my review when Payday 3 is at least as good as Payday 2.  If you care about Payday 3, then force Starbreeze to make it better by leaving a negative review to impact their sales.  Payday 3's issues since Day 1:  Barely any heists - We have only 16 (10 at launch) short heists with little replayability - Very little RNG elements in heists, especially non-dlc heists - Why can't we have Payday 2 level variety on how to complete heists (multiple escapes/breaches)? - Payday 2 has over 70 heists  No Multiday Heists - Payday 3 should have been released with at least 30 heists, 10 being multi-day  Most heists are short - I can solo most in under 12 mins on the hardest difficulty easily  Live Service only - Even Solo mode requires a ping before and after the game, often failing - Matchmaking was terrible, became bad and is terrible again now with crimenet - Want to host a lobby? Get ready to wait several minutes... - random disconnections, single lost network packet can disconnect you - 150ms lag feels like 600ms lag in any normal game - 5 min heist loading - UI freezes when loading, sometimes infinite - long loading if 1 player has bad internet - Sometimes instant disconnection after joining a lobby - Still have the can't ready up bug sometimes  Obvious game breaking bugs take forever to fix: - Cook off has had crashing issues since launch - Cook off escape van IS STILL BROKEN since 9 months ago (maybe fixed today) - Diamond District stuck door - Invisible enemies - Countless stuck objectives - UE4 is a decade old and very stable, bad Starbreeze code crashing game - It feels like little to no QA is done before releasing and update - Forget PD2 hotfixes, never had any  No heists with large maps - At least a 5/30 should be large maps  Few interesting heists - No real creativity for the jobs the gang does - Execution (ie drilling/hacking) is also pretty boring - Why are there still wifi circles after a whole year???  No Preplanning - Can only order basic assets without placing them  Many missing PD2 mechanics like driving  AI teammates are stupid - Cannot be instructed to wait and don't help with objectives - Sometimes don't even revive you if you are downed - Often broken/stuck (Fear & Greed) - Cannot have their loadouts, weapons or skills changed  Enemies are bland - Crowds of bullet sponge swat that do little damage to players - Only new special is the Nader (who was broken for most of this year) - Tasers are now called Zappers and are barely a threat - Only one variant of Bulldozer with an auto-shotgun - Bulldozers can only target a single player and are slow to retarget  Cloakers are too buggy - Attack through walls/corners - Always target players even if you're the last one - Can't be heard that well (were silent most of this year) - Can only be stopped by a one shot kill when they close charge you - Spawn from the same closest location every wave end so are easy to expect  Heisters to play as are lacking - Only 7 playable heisters, PD2 has 22 - Voicelines are bland and few - Barely any situational voicelines and no banter between heisters  Skills are super bland and boring - No drill skills, Inspire, Joker, Pistol Messiah or any interesting skills for that matter - I personally hate the new Armor plate economy, PD2 armor was perfect - No perk decks to make cool builds - No infamy system/rewards - Too easy to become 150, other players' renown levels hidden - Edge, Grit, Rush effects are an unnoticeable 10%  Music is acceptable but worse than PD2 - Only dlc heists have good music + Diamond District - No ability to change heists' music - No access to PD2/PDTH soundtrack  The UI is still bad - No number stats of weapons in UI, have to look these up online - Can't move guns around/sell preset guns - Weapons have same inventory icon regardless of which mods/skins are on it - Way too many large buttons/text which lead to unnecessary sub-pages and submenus - Can't manage party/other players directly from main menu/esc screen - No value of items shown when grabbed/secured - Separate control for marking than interacting unlike PD2 - Kicked to main menu after heist (can opt-in and wait 60s to form party which nobody does)  Few weapons - No melee weapons at all - No saw - Very few guns and the meta ones are all DLC - Same high fire rate and one shot kill weapons used for Overkill - No weapon lasers nor flashlights - Weapon sights cannot have the reticle changed at all - Secondary weapons all crazy suck except for DLC ones since Houston Breakout - No exciting weapons like Flamethrower, RPG or medieval weapons - Since day 1 we've begged to get rid of the OVK weapon system - No weapon skins that allow you to freely change mods  Few Throwables - Grenade barely does any damage - No cool throwables except the shock grenade - Only get 2 knives which break when hitting a wall  Few achievements: - Despite the little content, there should still be more achievements  Equipment sucks - Ecms are now useless, except disabling one camera you can reach - Infrasonic mines that you manually detonate to temporarily stun instead of tripmines - No c4 - Sentries are now a deployable so nobody uses them over Armor Bags  Few masks - Most masks are overdone and feel too ridiculous to ever wear - Very few patterns to choose from - Preset masks cannot be customized - No interesting materials - No cool masks compared to PD2  No Deathwish - Overkill is way too easy, never had to first play other difficulties - FBI van is stupid easy  Modifiers can't be changed - For the last few months, almost every heist has the 2 specials and blinding shield modifiers - I would like to enable all of them for more pay, they can't be changed  Pros over PD2 (this is why I would accept at least 30 heists vs PD2's 70 at launch) + Graphics gorgeous compared to PD2 but very unoptimized (ppl with 4080s are getting lag spikes) + Mask off stealth has more depth but now stealthing is way too easy + More heists with code locks which are cool to guess + Hostage system is better + Final Wave mechanic to make securing all loot harder + Fixed Naders are a cool threat + Bulldozers can melee kick and charge players + Cool animations when doing most interactions + Microcams/motion sensors useful and pretty cool + Shields have window to take them out through

For a game that had a promising lead into release, I was genuinely excited about the game. Something changed with the negative perception, made me realize I should temper my expectations. Then I actually got the game. Man, is this game a tragic story.  Payday 2 is true lightning in a bottle and everything they did with this was change for the sake of change, removing perk decks, making the Rush/Edge/Grit system a requirement to engage with any of your skills, the guns having little variety, including the instant meta that came to be that basically destroyed Overkill as "truly hard with no need for additional difficulties like PD2." I truly think this game isn't going to work out, and I believe that they're going to ditch it in the future to make more content for Payday 2.

Right now, the game just isn’t worth it. The core idea is fun, and the heists feel different from the first two games. Being able to sneak around without putting your mask on adds some really tense and exciting moments.  But the game’s got way too many issues holding it back. You need to be online to play — even in “offline” mode — and during heists you’ll often run into stutters or random disconnects. When that happens, you lose all your progress, which is beyond frustrating.  Maybe if they fix the servers and polish things up in the future, it could be worth giving another shot. But for now, I’d skip it.

Let me start by saying I played Payday 2 over a decade ago with my friends on a craptop, I remember when the first Hotline Miami crossover was new. While the gameplay and foundations are solid here, (hell I'd even say quite fun and far superior) the problem is the direction for the future is unsettling. Greedy, ignorant shareholders are already kneecapping this franchise and until there's a dedicated no internet required offline mode to keep the game alive past pulling the plug on the servers no one will want to spend any real money on this. I would gladly change my review to a positive one if that one thing was done and throw cash at more content but until then I'm looking at this as a temporary experience, a huge letdown, and keeping my wallet closed.

As someone who has been playing Payday 2 & 3 since launch, don't bother with this piece of ♥♥♥♥.   This game is boring, repetitive, lacking in content, flawed (online-only), and not improving at all aside from bugfixes & UI changes (it's been 2 years). The only compliments I can give this game are: Gustavo Coutinho's music is great, Jacket's Sociopath overskill weapon is fun, faces & hair don't look as scuffed compared to Payday 2, and the new Wolf VA is good. That's it. I had hope for this game, I wanted to give them a chance and fix & improve the game, "give them time" like some people will tell you, but it has been 2 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ years and nothing has improved, in fact it just gets worse and worse, especially after their recent announcement about cancelling offline mode. Basically if you have internet issues, or their servers ♥♥♥♥ up (again), you can't play the game you paid for. This game is basically a worse, subjectively "prettier" Payday 2 with less content, boring objectives, and some additional gimmicks.  Not to mention most of the new content in this game is already locked behind DLCs, and it was already this way during Year 1 which is pretty bad for a game with so many issues they should be focusing on instead of releasing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ DLCs. Starbreeze seems to be only interested in milking as much money as possible from the players with the least amount of effort (As seen with the recent Payday 2 subscription & DLC bundle price increase, also Delivery Charge is more expensive because... they felt like it?)  If I had the opportunity to refund this, I'd do it in a heartbeat. If you really want to play a Payday game, play Payday 2, Payday: The Heist (assuming they won't ruin those), or even Notoriety on Roblox instead. Stay FAR away from this game, and don't trust Starbreeze.

Wow, this game is a hot mess. So many things lost from Payday 2 when coming into this game, you question what's the point of a sequel when it has less content and its worse overall.  I won't go into detail given the countless negative reviews commenting on how this game sucks, so I'll just comment on my least favorite aspect of this game.  The cop animations are vastly inferior than Payday 2. In that game, cops reacted in a multitude of ways depending of the angles they're shot from. They would occasionally trip and fall, spending time to regain their footing. If shot on the back, they would throw their arms in surprise and fall forward. They even have an unique animation if you shot them in the balls. That energy is sorely missing in Payday 3. They brush off bullets as if they were airsoft pallets. They don't even throw themselves on the floor anymore to escape line of fire, they just occasionally sidestep and ragdoll on death.  No matter how much they rework Payday 3's skill or armor system like how they've been trying to do for over two years (holy ♥♥♥♥), this level of detail and energy on the cops is something we'll never see again.

Played this game at launch on Xbox Game Pass, and only came back for the 2nd anniversary discount. The improvements since then are noticeable and appreciated, but even with Starbreeze’s promising direction, Payday 3 still struggles with replayability as it gets dull after just a few runs. The maps feel like a jumble of reused assets rather than the creative playgrounds of PD1 and PD2. The new Delivery Charge heist does help by adding more RNG. But at the end of the day, it’s still just another warehouse heist.  Hosting options remain barebones. There’s no way to set infamy limits or properly shape your crew, so lobbies are a gamble. Even when I set a heist to Overkill, level 1 players can still join.  Honestly, the only reason I came back to this game is cuz I had fun playing with friends and have a bit of hope that the franchise will return to its former glory. (I will surely regret it later)

After 20 hours (including beta time) I have reached over lvl 100, I have bought most heist/weapon dlcs, and kept up with all the blogs, videos, etc. I can confidently say that this game is DISEASED with an out of touch company who have a very small amount of competency for the game they've created.  Here's the short of it  [h1] PROS [/h1]  -Gunplay that's SO GOOD I don't know if I'll be able to return to PD2's weapons  -Beautiful animations for guns, characters, and the new contextual ones for objectives  -Interesting setting with a large focus on technology and digital age gadgets/heists  -Cool new cops with updated designs and mechanics  -New "phases" for missions that'll make gameplay more progressive instead of stagnant for the entire level.  Yup, that's all the good. Ok here's the thing you're here for  [h1] CONS [/h1]  -The worst UI I've probably seen from any shooter let alone any game I've played Like for real THE GUNS AREN'T EVEN SEPARATED BY WEAPON TYPE! SECONDARIES ARE ALSO IN THE SAME SLOT AS PRIMARIES! HOW, HOW DO YOU F​U​C​K​I​N​G DO THIS??  -While the heists are mostly... ok. They don't have multi-day heists, escape heists, no bosses or unique enemies for the level. Most maps aren't randomized like in PD2. Unless you wanna count placement of keycards and minor stuff like that. Also cook-off isn't an infinite heist which blows p​e​n​i​s.  -I'm sure everyone's aware but I'll still put it here anyway. The skill system is dog s​h​i​t and revolves around 3 different modifiers. That being EDGE, GRIT, and RUSH. The idea of these 3 BEING in skills aren't the problem. It's just that the entire skill tree FOCUSES on these solely with like 5 individual skills actually giving you a unique mechanic. Just like the UI, it's super confusing.  -This game, while better than beta, is buggy as f​u​c​k. I promise you I am not suffering from mental r​e​t​a​r​d​a​t​i​o​n when I say I had a moment playing cook-off when the intercom ♥♥♥♥♥ told me to put in a chemical and I even waited for her to confirm it, yknow? Like wait for that second line that comes after it to make sure she didn't mess up the ingredients. I put it in AND THE F​UC​K​I​N​G LAB BLEW UP AFTER ONLY 5 BAGS.   -The team behind it wants to upgrade to Unreal Engine 5, but why? The engine they got now works just fine. Plus WHO WANTS THIS? I've heard literally NOBODY say anything positive about UE5. How about you actually add content?  -Guns are level-up progression now like in COD. It's ok... but I would rather just build my guns than grind for attachments. This locks players out of opportunities in heists because they hafta use a s​h​i​t weapon for 10 hours to unlock something that would make the weapon actually viable.                                                                     [LAST BUT NOT LEAST] Content. Why would I ever play PayDay 3 instead of PayDay 2? it suffers from a lack of Heists, weapons, customization, heisters, mechanics, etc etc etc. To be fair this isn't JUST a PayDay 3 issue, but it's still a valid criticism.  With no roadmap or updates from the devs, I can't say I have much faith in this game. At best it'll be "good" in 5 years after updates and DLCs but most people will forget about it by then.  Save your money.

As someone who bought the silver edition and stuck around since launch day, i have to say.    The game STILL isn't worth your money. does not matter what amount. its the worse version of a product you can have infinitely more fun with (and no, im not talking about the one with a subscription service added onto it.)    There are currently 16 heists in the game. This sounds good doesnt it, considering the standard the previous 2 games have set, right? Well, no, actually. There is little, if not ZERO randomisation in this game. No changes in objectives, no randomized events that could change the way you play a heist. Nothing. The very first game, PAYDAY: The Heist did this, and I've had way more fun with it than this zygote of a game.    Now lack of variety shouldn't really matter if the gameplay loop is fun, right? Wrong again! The game has tried to appeal to fans of both PAYDAY: The Heist and PAYDAY 2 by introducing a mixture of both the boots-on-the-ground gameplay where it feels like you're a nobody going up against the world, and the power fantasy of the second where it feels like youre a one man army. it fails on both of these fronts. Half of the reason why PAYDAY 2's power fantasy gameplay worked was because of build variety-- it never mattered how many cops you were slaughtering, but because you made something that could kill thousands of cops at a time.    And now they have told us that there will be no online mode whatsoever. You are buying Milk: the video game.    Buy PAYDAY The Heist and Postal 2 instead. you'll get infinitely more hours from those games.

Sadly, I can’t recommend this game anymore.  I wanted to like Payday 3 As someone who’s spent a decent amount playing Payday 2 I was hopeful it will be identical with better graphics and continuation of the story. But this game is a massive letdown, and now with the cancellation of offline mode, I’m done. So here’s my honest review:  Cons: - As of this review, they’ve announced that offline mode is cancelled. - Base game just feels trashy, running to objectives feels awkward, and combat just doesn't feel satisfying. It doesn’t feel satisfying to do anything. - The music is bad, unenjoyable, just background noise you’ll forget exists five minutes later (i had to download a jukebox mod with the payday 2 music just to have a better time). - The objectives are boring and not fun. You're telling me I have to stand in a circle to "catch Wi-Fi"? XD - The Edge Rush system is garbage. They mentioned a "Skill Points 2.0" update, but haven’t given a date, so who knows if or when that’s actually coming. - The voice lines for the character "Shade" sound cheap and robotic, almost like they were generated by text-to-speech, There's no personality, no intensity — just lifeless lines that take you out of the moment. - The special cops are just bullet sponges with zero personality, not challenging at all, and in general, Taking out cops in Payday 2 felt impactful, in Payday 3 it just isn't... - The new CEO, Adolf Kristjansson, previously worked at EA so you already know where this is going. - Microtransactions  Pros: - If you just pretend the Payday franchise doesn’t exist, or any heist game in that matter, like if this was the first ever heist game you might think it’s alright. -Stealth is fun (except for the circle objectives). - The graphics look good  Final Thoughts: The game is a complete mess. they’ve officially scrapped offline play,  That means when the servers go down and trust me, they will, the game will become completely unplayable. It's 2025 and we're still pretending always online is acceptable for a PvE co-op game? The only reason its online is because of the microtransactions. Trust me, this isn’t going to be like No Man’s Sky redemption arc. If you haven’t bought Payday 3 yet: don’t. If you already did, i hope you didn’t pay full price. And if you’re still holding on for a comeback... I respect your hope, but I wouldn’t count on it. All i can say is IT'S OVER. Its MY FAULT for having hopes for this game, i doubt i will change my review for this game unless most of the cons will be fixed.

This game is so bad. I really wanted to give it a chance, but it’s just terrible I don’t even feel excited to play it.  I waited for them to fix the game, maybe add map adjustments or a pre-planning system or something like that… but no. Instead, you have to complete another map just to unlock assets. Like, how dumb is that? I’ve got money and I want to spend it but nope.  Then there’s the dumb skill system, which I don’t even ****ing understand. You need to keep playing just to unlock another skill tree even though you already have skill points to spend. OMG, how does that make any sense? It’s just bad design.  There also aren’t many maps. Payday 2 had 11 maps at launch, and some even had Day 1, Day 2, and Day 3 variations. I loved doing Framing Frame with my friends in Payday 2 — I don’t even know how many times we finished it. But in Payday 3? I haven’t finished a single map in stealth because it’s so boring and unnecessarily hard. Like, you’re trying to rob a penthouse, and the map is tiny, yet there are a ton of security guards and cameras everywhere. It’s just frustrating instead of fun.  They even said they wouldn’t release Payday 2 maps — and then immediately broke that promise. Why? Because payday 3 maps are so boring.  And when you finish all the objectives, the game throws an endless assault at you that you can’t do anything about. It never stops. There are always cops shooting at you while you’re trying to get the bags to the van. It’s beyond frustrating.  There isn’t even a good story. In Payday 2, saving Hoxton actually felt cool. But here? You save Houston. Wow. What an original idea.  And even then, I still remember how amazing those Hoxton moments were in Payday 2. We were genuinely excited there were small things like Hoxton singing a Christmas song, little conversations about him and that big Dentist reveal. Those moments were awesome. Hoxton Breakout was such a hype event too.  If you’re going to copy and paste something, at least do it properly. You have great voice actors, but you don’t even use them well. Back then, they would release short story films and we’d watch them over and over again. Now? They don’t even bother doing that.
